You still need to trust that borrowers will repay on time and that fund managers will do their jobs. This technology does not protect you from bad loans. Therefore, when evaluating any private lending product (whether or not it's based on on-chain technology), the questions are simple: Who is borrowing? Why do they need this money? What collateral are these loans secured? How is the money recovered? Why is there this yield? If the answers to these questions are not clear, then abandon it. This is why private lending is so important to tokenization. It's about how to move one of the world's largest lending markets onto a more robust track.

data.title: January 2026 Market Review: Gold Strengthens, Crypto Assets Consolidate
data.description:
Author: Tanay Ved Source: Coin Metrics Translation: Shan Ouba, Jinse Finance

Key Takeaways

  • Geopolitical tensions drove funds into traditional safe-haven assets, with gold breaking through $5,000/ounce for the first time; Bitcoin rebounded to $97,000 mid-month, ultimately closing flat at the end of the month.

  • MicroStrategy added 37,215 Bitcoins (approximately $3.5 billion) in January, but ETF outflows at the end of the month coupled with rising market risk aversion put pressure on Bitcoin, preventing it from holding its highs.

  • MicroStrategy added 37,215 Bitcoins (approximately $3.5 billion) in January, but Bitcoin failed to hold its highs due to pressure from ETF outflows and rising market risk aversion at the end of the month.

  • Beneath the surface of the market, structural progress in the industry continues: Ethereum staking volume hit a record high (accounting for over 30% of circulating supply), the New York Stock Exchange announced the launch of its tokenization platform, and BitGo completed its IPO with a valuation of $2.6 billion. Introduction This article summarizes the dynamics of the cryptocurrency market and on-chain developments in January 2026. Market Performance Overview January started strongly, with Bitcoin rebounding to $97,000 and the total market capitalization of digital assets reaching approximately $3.4 trillion. However, influenced by geopolitical events, market momentum waned in the middle of the month. US actions regarding Greenland and renewed uncertainties in tariff policies with European countries suppressed risk assets, driving funds into the gold and precious metals markets. Market concerns about the security risks posed by quantum computing to Bitcoin, coupled with the stagnation of legislation related to the cryptocurrency market structure, have further exacerbated weak market sentiment. Meanwhile, gold continued its remarkable rally, breaking the $5,000/ounce mark for the first time in history. The performance of the top 100 crypto tokens by market capitalization is highly divergent, reflecting the current lack of a mainstream investment theme in the market. In Coin Metrics' industry classifications, the smart contract platform sector rose approximately 9.8% at the end of the month, closely followed by the value transfer token sector (up 4%). MicroStrategy continues to increase its holdings, leading to capital outflows from Bitcoin ETFs. MicroStrategy accumulated approximately 37,215 Bitcoins in January, including a large purchase of 22,305 Bitcoins, worth approximately $2.13 billion. This is the company's largest single purchase in US dollars since July 2025, bringing its total Bitcoin holdings to over 700,000. This increase was entirely financed through an market-to-market (ATM) offering of shares, rather than convertible bonds, and is part of the company's strategy to continuously increase its Bitcoin holdings per share. Supported by alternating increases in MicroStrategy holdings and inflows into Bitcoin ETFs, Bitcoin surged to the $97,000-$98,000 range in mid-January. However, in the latter half of January, risk aversion intensified in the macro market, with Bitcoin ETFs experiencing net outflows exceeding $1.2 billion in a single week, significantly diluting the marginal demand boost from MicroStrategy's increased holdings. It's noteworthy that the two sources of funds exhibited a stark contrast: ETF fund flows were highly sensitive to market momentum, while MicroStrategy maintained a strategic pace of accumulation regardless of price.

    Data Source: Coin Metrics

    Gold Rush

    Gold prices broke through $5,000/ounce for the first time, significantly outperforming Bitcoin in recent months. Bitcoin closed flat at the end of January, while gold rose by more than 18%, causing the Bitcoin/gold ratio to fall to a multi-year low.

    Data Source: Coin Metrics Price Data

    Gold's strength is driven by multiple factors:

    1. Geopolitical uncertainty: friction between the US and NATO over Greenland, the US's threat to reinstate tariffs on the EU and Canada, and market doubts about the independence of the Federal Reserve have all triggered market volatility, prompting funds to rotate towards safe-haven assets, flowing into traditional safe-haven assets, precious metals, and some emerging markets.

      Central banks worldwide continue to increase their gold reserves: Driven by rising US debt and the long-term trend of de-dollarization, global central banks continue to increase their gold reserves. Bitcoin's low correlation with gold: Despite Bitcoin's narrative as "digital gold," its rolling correlation with gold remains historically low, highlighting that Bitcoin has not followed gold in this safe-haven rally.

    Data source: Coin Metrics

    On-chain demand also confirms this trend: the total market capitalization of tokenized gold products such as Paxos' PAXG and Tether's XAUT recently exceeded $4.6 billion. Tether disclosed in its fourth-quarter XAUT holdings statement that the company purchased 27 tons of gold, a scale exceeding the gold purchases of most single-country central banks. ...>

    • Data Source: Coin Metrics

    Other Important Industry News

    Ethereum Staking Hits Record High

    Ethereum staking activity continues to heat up, with consensus layer staking exceeding 36.4 million tokens, and the staking ratio (the proportion of staking to circulating supply) rising to over 30%, further reducing the circulating supply.

    BitMine Immersion Technologies, the largest holder of Ethereum digital assets, disclosed that it has staked 2,009,267 Ethereum (worth $5.7 billion), nearly half of its total holdings (4.24 million, or 3.5% of the circulating supply).

    Data Source: Coin Metrics

    NYSE Launches Tokenization Platform

    The New York Stock Exchange announced plans to launch a security tokenization platform, providing blockchain-native trading services for US stocks and ETFs, supporting 24/7 trading, near-instant settlement, and using stablecoins as trading funds. Tokenized shares issued on this platform will remain fungible with listed stocks and retain core economic rights. BitGo Completes Initial Public Offering (IPO) The cryptocurrency IPO market is showing signs of recovery as BitGo listed on the New York Stock Exchange, with a first-day valuation of approximately $2.6 billion. Its stock price initially achieved double-digit gains before retreating, but the successful listing reflects public market demand for regulated cryptocurrency infrastructure companies. Pendle Adjusts Token Economic Model The yield-to-crypto decentralized finance protocol Pendle has switched from the vePENDLE model to a staking PENDLE model. This simplifies user participation while retaining the core incentive mechanisms surrounding protocol revenue and governance. This adjustment aligns with the reforms in fee mechanisms and value capture by protocols such as Uniswap and Aave, highlighting the industry's overall push for a more transparent and accessible transformation of token holders' economic rights. The legislation on the Crypto Asset Clarity Act has stalled. The legislative process related to the structure of the US digital asset market has slowed due to disagreements among parties regarding the regulation of stablecoin yields, leading to a postponement of the Senate's planned review of the bill. This delay leaves core issues concerning regulatory rules and oversight systems unresolved. In conclusion, in January 2026, Bitcoin opened higher and surged, but macroeconomic and geopolitical factors ultimately dominated the market's direction. Despite strong structural advantages for the industry, MicroStrategy's large-scale purchases at the beginning of the month failed to offset the outflow of funds and shrinking market liquidity at the end of the month, resulting in Bitcoin closing flat. Meanwhile, the growth in Ethereum staking, the NYSE's tokenization strategy, the successful listing of BitGo, and the continuous optimization of decentralized finance protocol token economic models all indicate that the cryptocurrency market infrastructure is maturing beneath the surface, laying the foundation for industry expansion after the macroeconomic environment stabilizes.
